facts.text: Belleair Shore was officially incorporated as a municipality in 1955, becoming one of Florida's smallest towns.
Belleair Shore lies on Gulf Boulevard’s west side, bordered by the Gulf to the west, Belleair Beach to the north and east, and Indian Rocks Beach to the south.
Belleair Shore is one of Florida’s smallest incorporated towns, covering just a few square miles.
Commercial and multi‑family high‑rise buildings are prohibited in Belleair Shore, with set‑back and height limits designed to protect its low‑rise character.
Residents of Belleair Shore enjoy unobstructed views of the western sky from their homes, a feature highlighted by the town’s coastal layout.
Belleair Shore lies four miles south of Clearwater Beach and one mile west of Belleair Bluffs, placing it within the Tampa Bay metropolitan area.
